truth.oneLiner: Salesforce REST API provides programmatic access to Salesforce org data and metadata for CRUD operations, queries, and business logic invocation.

record.name: Salesforce

final.name: Salesforce
final.oneLiner: Salesforce is the world's leading cloud CRM platform enabling sales, service, marketing, and agentic AI workflows via a unified Customer 360 data platform.

truth.oneLiner: Sherlock is an open-source command-line tool (not a hosted API) that finds usernames across social networks by querying sites directly from a local repository of definitions.
truth.blocker: No public HTTP API or developer endpoints are documented; Sherlock is a Python CLI application run locally, so there is no remote API to build an agent toolkit against.

record.name: Sherlock
record.oneLiner: Sherlock is an open-source command-line tool that finds usernames across many social networks and websites.
record.selfServeNote: It is a free open-source CLI project on GitHub with no account or gated access required to use or integrate.
record.apiSurface: No documented public REST/GraphQL API; it is a Python CLI library with functions that can be imported, no official MCP server exists.
record.blocker: No official hosted API or auth; would require wrapping the open-source CLI/library as a local tool rather than a standard SaaS API integration.

final.name: Sherlock
final.oneLiner: Sherlock is an open-source CLI tool to hunt down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks via local scraping.
final.selfServeNote: Fully self-serve and free; install via pipx/pip/uv/Docker or community packages (MIT licensed, no account or tier required) per GitHub README.
final.apiSurface: No documented public REST/GraphQL API; it is a Python CLI library (sherlock_project) run locally with no remote API, no MCP server or agent skills found in docs.
final.blocker: No hosted API or remote service; purely a local CLI/Python tool with no programmatic web API, auth, or endpoint to integrate as an agent toolkit without wrapping the CLI locally.

final.name: Paygent Connect
final.oneLiner: Paygent is a Japanese payment processing provider offering card, bank, carrier, convenience store, and B2B settlement services for e-commerce merchants.
final.selfServeNote: No self-serve signup or developer account; site only offers '資料請求' (document request) and 'お問い合わせ' (contact/inquiry) via web form or phone (03-6327-2862), indicating a sales/contact-gated merchant onboarding process with no public free/trial tier.
final.apiSurface: No public REST/GraphQL API documentation, auth specs, or MCP/agent skills found on the provided docs page; only marketing, case studies, and contact paths are present.
final.blocker: No publicly documented developer API or self-serve access; all integration requires contact-sales/partner gating with no visible technical docs.

truth.oneLiner: NotebookLM is not documented at the provided URL; the page covers the Gemini API on Google Cloud, not NotebookLM's developer interface.
truth.blocker: The provided developer docs (cloud.google.com/gemini) describe the Gemini API, not NotebookLM. No NotebookLM API, endpoints, or auth specifics are present, so the app cannot be assessed or built from these docs.

record.name: NotebookLM
record.oneLiner: NotebookLM is a Google AI tool that turns user-provided documents into a conversational research and summarization assistant.
record.selfServeNote: NotebookLM is a consumer Google product without a publicly self-serve developer API; access is controlled by Google and not openly registrable for third-party API use.
record.apiSurface: No documented public REST/GraphQL API or MCP for NotebookLM; only web UI exists. The hint URL points to Gemini/Google Cloud which is separate and has its own APIs.
record.blocker: No public API or developer program for NotebookLM; cannot build agent toolkit without undocumented/internal access.

final.name: NotebookLM
final.oneLiner: NotebookLM is Google's AI research and note-based synthesis assistant (not documented at the Gemini cloud page, which covers Gemini models/APIs and enterprise agents).
final.selfServeNote: No NotebookLM developer access, API, or self-serve tier documented at the provided Gemini docs URL; only Gemini Developer API (no-cost key) and enterprise/partner programs are described, so NotebookLM build access is undocumented/gated.
final.apiSurface: No public REST/GraphQL API, MCP server, or agent skills for NotebookLM found in the retrieved Gemini docs; page documents Gemini Developer API, Agent Platform, and Workspace/Gemini enterprise apps only.

truth.oneLiner: Mermaid CLI (mmdc) is a Node.js command-line tool that renders Mermaid diagrams to SVG/PNG/PDF from the terminal or scripts, not a hosted HTTP API.
truth.blocker: No public HTTP API or remote service endpoints are documented; it is a local CLI executable requiring installation via npm and run locally, so it cannot be wrapped as a remote agent toolkit API from the docs.

record.name: Mermaid CLI
record.oneLiner: Command-line tool to render Mermaid diagrams (markdown-like syntax) to SVG/PNG/PDF via Puppeteer.
record.selfServeNote: Open-source CLI distributed via npm, no account or approval needed to install and run locally.
record.apiSurface: No hosted REST/GraphQL API; it is a local Node.js CLI (mmdc) with documented flags and config; no existing MCP server in repo.
record.blocker: No remote API or auth; would need a wrapper service or local execution bridge to expose as an agent tool.

final.name: Mermaid CLI
final.oneLiner: Mermaid CLI (mmdc) is an open-source command-line tool that converts Mermaid diagram definition files into SVG, PNG, or PDF output.
final.selfServeNote: Fully open-source and self-serve via npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli or Docker pull; no account, tier, or payment required (MIT-licensed, public GitHub repo).
final.apiSurface: No REST/GraphQL API; it is a local CLI executable (mmdc) with stdin/file input and SVG/PNG/PDF output. No documented MCP server or agent skills; only a Node.js CLI and Docker image.
final.blocker: No network-accessible API surface (only local CLI/Docker); requires process execution or container wrapping rather than standard HTTP API integration for agent toolkit.
final.evidence: https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli (README.md: 'This is a command-line interface (CLI) for mermaid. It takes a mermaid definition file as input and generates an SVG/PNG/PDF file as output.'; install via npm/Docker; usage mmdc -i input.mmd -o output.svg)
